tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text: {"number":57822,"title":"[Identity] Docs: ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md snippets out of sync with .NET 9 API updates","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Identity` **Service directory:** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/` **Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #57522) by `@fowl2` ## What Changed PR #57522 enhanced `ClientCertificateCredential` documentation with platform certificate store support. In `CertificateCredentialSnippets.cs`, two snippets were updated to use the recommended .NET 9+ API (`X509CertificateLoader`) via `#if NET9_0_OR_GREATER` conditional blocks: - `Identity_CertificateCredential_CreateWithX509Cert` — now uses `X509CertificateLoader.LoadPkcs12FromFile` on .NET 9+ - `Identity_CertificateCredential_RotatingCredential` — now uses `X509CertificateLoader.LoadCertificateFromFile` on .NET 9+ However, `Update-Snippets.ps1` was not run after these test file changes, so `ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md` still shows the old `.NET 8` form without the `#if NET9_0_OR_GREATER` conditional branches. ## Gaps Found - `ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md` snippet `Identity_CertificateCredential_CreateWithX509Cert` shows `new X509Certificate2(...)` but the backing test code now uses `X509CertificateLoader.LoadPkcs12FromFile` on .NET 9+ - `ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md` snippet `Identity_CertificateCredential_RotatingCredential` shows `new X509Certificate2(_path)` but the backing test code now uses `X509CertificateLoader.LoadCertificateFromFile` on .NET 9+ - Users on .NET 9+ are directed to a deprecated API (`X509Certificate2` constructor with path) rather than the recommended `X509CertificateLoader` API - The `Identity_CertificateCredential_CreateFromStore` snippet references `CertificateNotFoundException` without any explanation that this is a user-defined exception type — readers may be confused about where it comes from 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above ### Step 1: Modify files No files need to be manually edited. The snippet content is generated automatically from `CertificateCredentialSnippets.cs` via `Update-Snippets.ps1`. Simply run that script to sync the markdown. However, the `CreateFromStore` snippet gap requires a manual prose change in the markdown. **File to edit:** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/samples/ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md` In the "Loading certificates from an X509Store" section, after the `CreateFromStore` snippet block, add a note explaining `CertificateNotFoundException`: ```markdown > **Note:** `CertificateNotFoundException` in the example above is a user-defined exception. You can replace it with any appropriate exception type (such as `InvalidOperationException`) or define your own: > > ```csharp > public sealed class CertificateNotFoundException(string message) : Exception(message) { } > ``` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets The snippet content for `Identity_CertificateCredential_CreateWithX509Cert` and `Identity_CertificateCredential_RotatingCredential` must be regenerated from the test source file. The test file already contains the correct `#if NET9_0_OR_GREATER` branches — just run the snippet update tooling. **Test file:** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/tests/samples/CertificateCredentialSnippets.cs` The relevant snippets already contain the correct code with `#if NET9_0_OR_GREATER` / `#else` / `#endif` blocks. **README/Samples location:** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/samples/ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md` After running `Update-Snippets.ps1`, verify that the following snippet placeholders in the markdown were updated to include the `#if NET9_0_OR_GREATER` block: - `` ```C# Snippet:Identity_CertificateCredential_CreateWithX509Cert `` - `` ```C# Snippet:Identity_CertificateCredential_RotatingCredential `` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The `ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md` is a standalone samples doc (not the main README). Its current structure covers the required scenarios. No structural changes are needed beyond the snippet sync and `CertificateNotFoundException` clarification. The main `README.md` at `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/README.md` already has all required sections. No changes needed there. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next: 1. `dotnet build sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 identity` — regenerates snippet blocks in markdown from `#region Snippet:` tags in test files. After running, check the git diff on `ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md` to confirm the `#if NET9_0_OR_GREATER` blocks now appear in the snippet output 4. `dotnet format sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24047751321/agentic_workflow) · ● 486.6K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57822","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-06T19:48:10Z","updated_at":"2026-04-06T19:48:10Z"}

{"number":57822,"title":"[Identity] Docs: ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md snippets out of sync with .NET 9 API updates","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Identity` **Service directory:** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/` **Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #57522) by `@fowl2` ## What Changed PR #57522 enhanced `ClientCertificateCredential` documentation with platform certificate store support. In `CertificateCredentialSnippets.cs`, two snippets were updated to use the recommended .NET 9+ API (`X509CertificateLoader`) via `#if NET9_0_OR_GREATER` conditional blocks: - `Identity_CertificateCredential_CreateWithX509Cert` — now uses `X509CertificateLoader.LoadPkcs12FromFile` on .NET 9+ - `Identity_CertificateCredential_RotatingCredential` — now uses `X509CertificateLoader.LoadCertificateFromFile` on .NET 9+ However, `Update-Snippets.ps1` was not run after these test file changes, so `ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md` still shows the old `.NET 8` form without the `#if NET9_0_OR_GREATER` conditional branches. ## Gaps Found - `ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md` snippet `Identity_CertificateCredential_CreateWithX509Cert` shows `new X509Certificate2(...)` but the backing test code now uses `X509CertificateLoader.LoadPkcs12FromFile` on .NET 9+ - `ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md` snippet `Identity_CertificateCredential_RotatingCredential` shows `new X509Certificate2(_path)` but the backing test code now uses `X509CertificateLoader.LoadCertificateFromFile` on .NET 9+ - Users on .NET 9+ are directed to a deprecated API (`X509Certificate2` constructor with path) rather than the recommended `X509CertificateLoader` API - The `Identity_CertificateCredential_CreateFromStore` snippet references `CertificateNotFoundException` without any explanation that this is a user-defined exception type — readers may be confused about where it comes from 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above ### Step 1: Modify files No files need to be manually edited. The snippet content is generated automatically from `CertificateCredentialSnippets.cs` via `Update-Snippets.ps1`. Simply run that script to sync the markdown. However, the `CreateFromStore` snippet gap requires a manual prose change in the markdown. **File to edit:** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/samples/ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md` In the "Loading certificates from an X509Store" section, after the `CreateFromStore` snippet block, add a note explaining `CertificateNotFoundException`: ```markdown > **Note:** `CertificateNotFoundException` in the example above is a user-defined exception. You can replace it with any appropriate exception type (such as `InvalidOperationException`) or define your own: > > ```csharp > public sealed class CertificateNotFoundException(string message) : Exception(message) { } > ``` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets The snippet content for `Identity_CertificateCredential_CreateWithX509Cert` and `Identity_CertificateCredential_RotatingCredential` must be regenerated from the test source file. The test file already contains the correct `#if NET9_0_OR_GREATER` branches — just run the snippet update tooling. **Test file:** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/tests/samples/CertificateCredentialSnippets.cs` The relevant snippets already contain the correct code with `#if NET9_0_OR_GREATER` / `#else` / `#endif` blocks. **README/Samples location:** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/samples/ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md` After running `Update-Snippets.ps1`, verify that the following snippet placeholders in the markdown were updated to include the `#if NET9_0_OR_GREATER` block: - `` ```C# Snippet:Identity_CertificateCredential_CreateWithX509Cert `` - `` ```C# Snippet:Identity_CertificateCredential_RotatingCredential `` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The `ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md` is a standalone samples doc (not the main README). Its current structure covers the required scenarios. No structural changes are needed beyond the snippet sync and `CertificateNotFoundException` clarification. The main `README.md` at `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/README.md` already has all required sections. No changes needed there. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next: 1. `dotnet build sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 identity` — regenerates snippet blocks in markdown from `#region Snippet:` tags in test files. After running, check the git diff on `ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md` to confirm the `#if NET9_0_OR_GREATER` blocks now appear in the snippet output 4. `dotnet format sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24047751321/agentic_workflow) · ● 486.6K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57822","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-06T19:48:10Z","updated_at":"2026-04-06T19:48:10Z"}

{"total_count":2,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":911354090,"number":21584,"state":"closed","state_reason":"completed","locked":true,"title":"[BUG] No distributed traces for Azure.Storage.Blobs and Azure.Messaging.ServiceBus when running in Azure Functions","body":"**Describe the bug**

I can find some traces for the following SDKs:

- Azure table (used by durable functions)
- Old cosmos SDK (3.19)

However I am heavy user of Azure.Storage.Blobs (12.8.4) but my code is running in Azure Function v3 so I guess the old SDK is in the dependency tree somewhere.

**Expected behavior**

I expect to see information about my blob traffic.

**Actual behavior (include Exception or Stack Trace)**

no dependency metrics for storage blobs

**To Reproduce**

Run the code.

```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>netcoreapp3.1</TargetFramework>
    <AzureFunctionsVersion>V3</AzureFunctionsVersion>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Azure.Identity" Version="1.4.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="Azure.Messaging.EventHubs" Version="5.4.1" />
    <PackageReference Include="Azure.Messaging.ServiceBus" Version="7.1.2" />
    <PackageReference Include="Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets" Version="4.1.1" />
    <PackageReference Include="Azure.Storage.Blobs" Version="12.8.4" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Azure.Cosmos" Version="3.19.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Azure.Devices" Version="1.34.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Azure.Functions.Extensions" Version="1.1.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Azure.ServiceBus" Version="5.1.3" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Azure.WebJobs.Extensions.DurableTask" Version="2.5.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Azure.WebJobs.Extensions.ServiceBus" Version="4.3.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Azure.WebJobs.Extensions" Version="4.0.1" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Azure.WebJobs.Logging.ApplicationInsights" Version="3.0.27" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Azure.WebJobs" Version="3.0.27" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.Abstractions" Version="3.1.11" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging" Version="3.1.11" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Functions" Version="3.0.13" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.ApplicationInsights" Version="2.17.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Cur8.Core.Typelib" Version="$(CoreLibraryVersion)" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <None Update="host.json">
      <CopyToOutputDirectory>PreserveNewest</CopyToOutputDirectory>
    </None>
    <None Update="local.settings.json">
      <CopyToOutputDirectory>PreserveNewest</CopyToOutputDirectory>
      <CopyToPublishDirectory>Never</CopyToPublishDirectory>
    </None>
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <ProjectReference Include="..\DeviceBackend.Dal\DeviceBackend.Dal.csproj" />
    <ProjectReference Include="..\DeviceBackend.Recording\DeviceBackend.Recording.csproj" />
    <ProjectReference Include="..\..\src\OpenSslWrapper\OpenSslWrapper.csproj" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

**Environment:**
 - Azure.Storage.Blobs 12.8.4
 - Azure Function V3
 - dotnet core 3.1

Failing to authenticate using ClientSecretCredentials produces ObjectDisposedException instead of a meaningful error message

**Expected behavior**
A meaningful error message is produced

**Actual behavior (include Exception or Stack Trace)**

arguments.message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member best able to assist

arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** The `ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md` snippets are out of sync with changes from PR #57522, which updated `CertificateCredentialSnippets.cs` to use the .NET 9+ `X509CertificateLoader` API. Running `Update-Snippets.ps1` and adding a clarification note for `CertificateNotFoundException` will resolve the gaps.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.Identity`
**Affected API:** `ClientCertificateCredential` documentation snippets (`ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md`)
**Scenarios:**
- `Identity_CertificateCredential_CreateWithX509Cert` snippet shows old `new X509Certificate2(...)` instead of `X509CertificateLoader.LoadPkcs12FromFile` on .NET 9+
- `Identity_CertificateCredential_RotatingCredential` snippet shows `new X509Certificate2(_path)` instead of `X509CertificateLoader.LoadCertificateFromFile` on .NET 9+
- `Identity_CertificateCredential_CreateFromStore` references `CertificateNotFoundException` without explanation that it is a user-defined type

**Root ask:** Sync the markdown snippets with the updated test source (run `Update-Snippets.ps1 identity`) and add a clarifying prose note about `CertificateNotFoundException` in the samples doc.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

The backing test file `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/tests/samples/CertificateCredentialSnippets.cs` already has the correct `#if NET9_0_OR_GREATER` / `#else` / `#endif` blocks from PR #57522 commit `800a7825`. The markdown doc was not regenerated after that commit, so the snippet content in `ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md` is stale.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 identity` and inspect the git diff on `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/samples/ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md` — the two affected snippets should now include `#if NET9_0_OR_GREATER` blocks.
2. Manually add a prose note after the `CreateFromStore` snippet explaining that `CertificateNotFoundException` is a user-defined type (example definition provided in the issue).
3. Run `dotnet build sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/` and `dotnet test sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` to verify nothing is broken.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — `Azure.Identity` is a data-plane client library (not a management or provisioning package)
- **Service:** `Azure.Identity` — the issue explicitly names the `Azure.Identity` package and its `sdk/identity/` directory
- **Confidence:** High — the issue unambiguously targets `Azure.Identity` with a clear client-library documentation gap; no other service or category is plausible
</details>
